Curated Chaos · Hero Intro · Full Storyboard

Fragments of Real People

A scroll-driven title sequence: he casts, the universe shatters into black glass, six fragments reveal on their own motif, the fight escalates to the sun — then the blinder, the black, and the invitation to make your own.

Shots14 Keyframes11 locked GradeGold & black · grainy analog Continuity@Husam + @HusamHero, face-audited DeliveryKeyframes → video scenes
scroll-scrubbed motion shard-wipe (no hard cut) eye-blinder flash → black

The Shatter

Act II

The Six Fragments

Shots 03–08
03

Fragment I — Agustín

“A minimalist visual poem about artistic awakening.”

Motif / action
The disc itself — its vinyl-record shape and colour is his motif, already native to the disc-magic.
Keyframe
K1 · the disc

Shard-wipe
04

Fragment II — Najoua

“Kindness mistaken for weakness — until she spoke.”

Motif / action
Lotus / petals — glimpsed fast, reflected in a tumbling shard. Never a slow standalone scene (tempo rule).
Keyframe
K11 · 64b1226c

Shard-wipe
05

Fragment III — Başak

“From protective invisibility to deliberate presence.”

Motif / action
Blue evil-eye (nazar) in blue smoke, held at the golden-ratio focal point — the damage beat before Flame Inside.

K9 · e2118e76

Shard-wipe
06

Fragment IV — Yaşar Efe

“The figure who returns the blade to himself.”

Motif / action
Ursa Major / Ursa Minor constellation glowing in the sky beside the sun, seen as he charges up — low heroic angle.

07

Fragment V — Baver

“A year of surviving panic without yielding.”

Motif / action
Glass-only interstitial — purple neon smoke and green digital-data read in the glass. He does not appear.

08

Fragment VI — Federica · Espera

“Foglia d'Oro — the gold leaf beneath her skin.”

Motif / action
The lapis pendant falls from a disc-gate; he lunges forward to catch it as a second gate behind shows only the chain — teleported back to hand.
Keyframe
K8 · c6a5f493

Act IV

The Close

Shots 13–14
13

Eye-blinder → black

— no on-screen text —

Motif / action
The sun whites out the whole frame, then the whiteout resolves straight to black — no bright gap. (Verified: flash peaks 0.99 → clean handoff to full blackout.)
Keyframe
Site effect
Transition in
White flash → blackout
14
Dark &
fantastical
elements.

Call to action

“Dark & fantastical elements. — Create your own fragment →”

Motif / action
The button launches the playbook (Phase 0), where the viewer begins their own fragment.
Keyframe
Over black
Transition in
Fades up over black

These stills are the video's scene frames. Each locked keyframe is the source frame for a hero-video clip (image → video, next phase); the site plays the video scenes, not static images. Keyframe-first, then motion — the playbook order.

Tempo rule. The delicate motifs — lotus, evil-eye, constellation, data — are delivered fast: split-second close-ups on the shard-wipes and reflections in the tumbling glass, never slow standalone scenes that break the action tempo. Only the pendant-catch and Başak's damage beat are full dynamic scenes.

The ultimate is the closer. Sun-burst → eye-blinder white flash → fade to black → CTA. The button launches the playbook at Phase 0, where the viewer begins their own fragment.

Curated Chaos — 360° continuity of character, object, location
